channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Nobel prize for A-bomb survivors' group boosts hope for Japanese seeking a nuke-free world
item.description: For many Japanese survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki and their relatives, Friday’s awarding of the Nobel Peace Prize to a Japanese organization against nuclear weapons gives them hope for a new momentum to push for a nuclear-free world.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Zelenskyy appeals to allies to keep up aid as Germany pledges new weapons package
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday it's important that allies' aid to Ukraine doesn't decrease next year as he received a pledge of a new weapons package from German Chancellor Olaf Scholz on a European tour meant to win backing for his “victory plan” aimed at ending the war wit
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken tells ASEAN the US is worried about China's 'dangerous' actions in disputed sea.
item.description: VIENTIANE, Laos (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken told Southeast Asian leaders Friday that the U.S. is concerned about China's “increasingly dangerous and unlawful” activities in the disputed South China Sea during an annual summit meeting, and pledged the U.S.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Archaeologists unearth well-preserved 4,000-year-old Egyptian tomb
item.description: A joint Egyptian-German archaeological mission found the tomb of an ancient governor's daughter. Two tombs were found, one inside the other, covered in intricate script and art.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In separate strikes, Israeli forces kill 2 Lebanese soldiers and injure 2 UN peacekeepers
item.description: The Lebanese army says that an Israeli airstrike killed two Lebanese soldiers and wounded three other troops on Friday, an incident that entangles the country’s official army in the escalating conflict between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah militants in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia launches 4th aerial attack in a week against Ukraine's grain-exporting Odesa region
item.description: Authorities say a nighttime Russian missile strike on Odesa has killed at least four people, including a 16-year-old girl.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize given to Japanese organization Nihon Hidankyo for its work against nuclear weapons
item.description: The Nobel Peace Prize has been awarded to Nihon Hidankyo, a Japanese organization of survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, for its activism against nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Mike Corder And Elena Becatoros, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Zealand ship didn’t sink because its captain was a woman, the ‘appalled’ defense minister says
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — New Zealand's defense minister issued stinging rebukes of what she said were “vile” and “misogynistic” online remarks by “armchair admirals” about the woman captain of a navy ship that ran aground, caught fire and sank off the coast of Samoa.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy city-hops across Europe, promoting 'victory plan' to allies
item.description: ROME (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was city-hopping across Europe on Thursday to promote a “victory plan” that he said “aims to create the right conditions for a just end to the war" against Russia, detailing the proposals to European allies after a summit with President Joe Biden w
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dozens wounded, at least 22 killed in airstrikes in Beirut as tensions escalate in Israel conflict with Iran
item.description: At least 22 people were killed and dozens of others left wounded in airstrikes in central Beirut, further escalating Israel’s deadly conflict with Iran-backed Hezbollah militants in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British YouTuber and rapper Yung Filly is charged with raping and choking a woman in Australia
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — A British YouTuber and rapper known as Yung Filly has been charged with raping and choking a woman in a hotel room following an Australian music performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rome mayor responds after Macron says he'll fight to keep 'Emily in Paris' in France: 'Netflix does not take orders from heads of state'
item.description: "Emily in Paris" is sparking a très dramatique back-and-forth between the French president and the mayor of Rome, who are battling about the Netflix series potentially bidding adieu to the City of Light.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: UN: Israeli military fired on peacekeepers in southern Lebanon
item.description: The United Nations on Thursday accused the Israeli military of firing on peacekeeper missions in southern Lebanon as Israel conducts a cross-border operation inside the country.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Archaeologists unearth unexpected find inside a tomb likely belonging to a Roman gladiator
item.description: In Turkey, archaeologists unearthed a tomb seemingly belonging to a Roman gladiator of the third century B.C., but were surprised to find the remains of 12 individuals inside.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel seeks to reshape Middle East, with force and US backing
item.description: One year after Hamas’s Oct. 7 attack, Israel is reshaping the Middle East by force, taking the fight to Iran and its proxies with the help of the United States — despite pleas from the Biden administration for diplomacy.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: With roads often blocked by rebels in Congo, boats — the only alternative — have become death traps
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — The overcrowded boat that capsized in eastern Congo last week killed eight members of Serge Nzonga’s family along with 70 others. Days later, he was back on the same route that claimed their lives in yet another boat lacking safety measures.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and RUTH ALONGA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden, Netanyahu speak ahead of expected retaliation against Iran
item.description: President Biden spoke with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Wednesday for the first time in weeks, amid heightened tensions between the leaders as Israel calibrates its expected retaliatory strike against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Susan Rice: Trump's reported calls with Putin appear illegal
item.description: Former White House adviser and United Nations ambassador Susan Rice called out former President Trump over a report that he has spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin at least seven times since leaving office, suggesting such calls would violate the Logan Act.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: UN tribunal orders ex-official to repay $58.8 million lost in bad deals while getting perks
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — A U.N. tribunal has ordered a former high-ranking official to repay the United Nations $58.8 million lost in deals he made with a British businessman who showered him with interest-free loans, a Mercedes for his wife and perks for his sons.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The history of the Hoxne Hoard, the largest collection of Roman treasure found in Britain
item.description: The Hoxne Hoard is one of the most significant archaeological finds out of Britain. The hoard is mostly made of gold and silver coins, although other unique objects add to its value.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID-19 tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic, as was revealed in a new book by journalist Bob Woodward, Bloomberg reported.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Macron vows to 'fight hard' to bring Netflix's 'Emily in Paris' back to France
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is on a diplomatic mission to return "Emily in Paris" back to the City of Light.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: Google loses final EU court appeal against 2.4 billion euro fine in antitrust shopping case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google lost its final legal challenge on Tuesday against a European Union penalty for giving its own shopping recommendations an illegal advantage over rivals in search results, ending a long-running antitrust case that came with a whopping fine.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: British doctor admits to attempted murder after injecting mother’s partner with poison disguised as vaccine
item.description: British doctor, Thomas Kwan, pleaded guilty to an attempted murder charge after 'overwhelming' evidence showed he administer a poisonous fake COVID vaccination.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FBI arrests Afghan man who officials say planned Election Day attack in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI has arrested an Afghan man who officials say was inspired by the Islamic State militant organization and was plotting an Election Day attack targeting large crowds in the U.S., the Justice Department said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hungary’s Orban interrupted, accused of 'selling out' country to Russia, China during EU news conference
item.description: Hungary's Prime Minister Viktor Orban was interrupted at a news conference by an activist and municipal councilor from the opposition Democratic Coalition party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican mayor's severed head placed atop pickup truck 6 days after taking office
item.description: The mayor of a violence-plagued city in Mexico was killed on Sunday with his decapitated body left in a pickup truck and his severed head placed atop the vehicle's roof.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea's Kim again threatens to use nuclear weapons against South Korea and US
item.description: The latest warning came as experts say North Korea could ramp up hostilities ahead of the U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Four American hostages remain in Hamas captivity a year after Oct. 7 massacre
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is vowing that the U.S. "will continue to work tirelessly" to bring home Americans being held hostage by Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How many are dead, missing one year since Hamas' attack on Israel?
item.description: Oct. 7 marks the first anniversary of Hamas’ attack on Israel, which prompted an Israeli invasion of Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Russia jails American Stephen Hubbard over fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine
item.description: Stephen Hubbard, 72, of Michigan, has been sentenced by a Russian court Monday after being convicted of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abigail Edan's aunt says 'we need a deal' on Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: "I say this to the Palestinian people, and I say this to the Israelis Diplomacy now is what we need," Naftali said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: A strike on a mosque kills at least 19, Palestinian officials say, as Israel bombards northern Gaza and southern Lebanon
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a mosque in the Gaza Strip early Sunday killed at least 19 people, Palestinian officials said, as Israel intensified its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Lebanon in a widening war with Iran-allied militant groups across the region.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Relatives say a whole family was killed in Israel's deadliest West Bank strike since Oct. 7
item.description: TULKAREM, West Bank (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a West Bank cafe that the military said targeted Palestinian militants also killed a family of four, including two young children, relatives told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: NASSER NASSER and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, WGN 9
